_meta.description: Names that LLMs frequently emit as `pip install X` or `npm install X` but are actually language builtins/stdlib. Used by /check_package, /package_exists and /ai_brief to return an educational hint instead of a bare 404.

urllib2.note: Python 2 stdlib module. Removed in Python 3 — use urllib.request for simple fetches or the `requests` package for ergonomic HTTP.

urlparse.note: Python 2 stdlib module. In Python 3, import from `urllib.parse`.

httplib.note: Python 2 stdlib module. In Python 3, use `http.client`.

ConfigParser.note: Python 2 stdlib. In Python 3 the module is lowercase `configparser`.

Queue.note: Python 2 stdlib. In Python 3 use lowercase `queue`.

thread.note: Python 2 low-level thread module. In Python 3 it is `_thread`; for most code use `threading`.

cStringIO.note: Python 2 only. In Python 3 use `io.StringIO` (text) or `io.BytesIO` (bytes).

fs.note: Node.js core module. Use `import fs from 'node:fs'` or `const fs = require('fs')` — no npm install required.

flutter.replacement: Use Flutter SDK directly (https://flutter.dev). Do NOT add 'flutter' as a dep in pubspec.yaml — use `flutter create`/`flutter pub`.
flutter.note: Flutter is the framework itself, not a pub package. The 'flutter' pub package on pub.dev is a deprecated 2016 placeholder.

sky_engine.replacement: `sky_engine` ships with the Dart/Flutter SDK — no install required.

dart.replacement: `dart` ships with the Dart/Flutter SDK — no install required.

SwiftUI.replacement: import SwiftUI — bundled with iOS/macOS SDK, no SwiftPM dep needed.
SwiftUI.note: Apple system framework. Ships with the OS SDK, not a Swift Package Manager package.
